        feat_sam2_foreground_segmentation["<b>feat/sam2-foreground-segmentation</b><br/>Replace feature-clustering<br/>remove_bg with SAM2<br/>lightweight segmentation<br/>(auto DINO-saliency seed +<br/>/ - click refine, temporal<br/>propagation, mask baked<br/>into PCA video)"]
        feat_sam_autoseed_quality["<b>feat/sam-autoseed-quality</b><br/>Improve SAM2 auto-seed:<br/>single DINO-saliency point<br/>misses on some frames; try<br/>multi-point / better<br/>saliency / SAM auto-mask-<br/>gen fallback"]
        perf_sam_session_cache["<b>perf/sam-session-cache</b><br/>Persist the<br/>Sam2VideoInferenceSession<br/>/ vision features per run<br/>so +/- click re-<br/>segmentation skips<br/>recomputation and cuts<br/>click latency"]
        fix_segment_click_frame_idx["<b>fix/segment-click-frame-idx</b><br/>Segment refine clicks<br/>always seed frame 0:<br/>client sends [x,y,label]<br/>with no frame, sam.segment<br/>uses seed_frame=0 -&gt; a<br/>click on a later frame<br/>maps coords to frame 0 and<br/>corrupts SAM propagation.<br/>Thread frame idx (point<br/>schema + per-frame<br/>prompting)"]
        fix_sam_failure_silent_fallback["<b>fix/sam-failure-silent-fallback</b><br/>SAM2 exception/empty mask<br/>is hidden as all-<br/>foreground +<br/>available=False, which<br/>also strips the client's<br/>click controls (no<br/>recovery) and lets refine<br/>failures overwrite good<br/>masks; distinguish error<br/>vs empty, keep controls,<br/>don't clobber on refine<br/>failure, add failure-mode<br/>tests (monkeypatch + mask-<br/>ratio asserts)"]
        fix_shared_model_load_lock["<b>fix/shared-model-load-lock</b><br/>extractor and SAM2<br/>from_pretrained race on<br/>torch global default<br/>dtype: their _load_lock<br/>objects are separate and<br/>run_group warms both<br/>concurrently (pipeline<br/>534-535); share ONE global<br/>model-construction lock or<br/>sequence extractor-&gt;SAM<br/>warm"]
        fix_segment_point_validation["<b>fix/segment-point-validation</b><br/>_parse_points accepts<br/>NaN/Infinity/out-of-bounds<br/>coords (shape-only check);<br/>validate finite + within<br/>the run's WxH (+ frame<br/>idx) and reject 4xx before<br/>reaching SAM2"]
        fix_run_mutation_mutex["<b>fix/run-mutation-mutex</b><br/>segment/refit re-render is<br/>not excluded from DELETE<br/>/api/runs, DELETE<br/>/api/sources, or<br/>(source,model) supersede —<br/>a concurrent delete can<br/>rmtree<br/>run_dir/feats/masks/source<br/>mid-render; track in-<br/>progress run mutations and<br/>gate destructive ops"]
        spike_frame_alignment_check["<b>spike/frame-alignment-check</b><br/>Verify 'frame alignment is<br/>exact': phase-1 GPU-decode<br/>vs SAM CPU re-decode both<br/>use seek_mode=approximate<br/>and tests only check<br/>index-length; add<br/>checksum/hash verification<br/>+ open-GOP/VFR fixtures,<br/>or reuse one decode path"]
        decision_refit_mask_grid_threshold["<b>decision/refit-mask-grid-threshold</b><br/>Is refit_and_render's adap<br/>tive_avg_pool2d(mask,grid)<br/>&gt;0.5 (drops &lt;50%-fg<br/>patches, excluding thin<br/>arms/tools/wheels from the<br/>color refit) intended, or<br/>should it use a lower<br/>threshold / soft weighting<br/>to cover thin structures?"]
        fix_refit_soft_weight_mask["<b>fix/refit-soft-weight-mask</b><br/>Replace refit's hard adapt<br/>ive_avg_pool2d(mask,grid)&<br/>gt;0.5 fg-token gate with<br/>fg-fraction SOFT WEIGHTING<br/>(weighted PCA over grid<br/>tokens) so thin structures<br/>survive the color refit;<br/>no hardcoded threshold, no<br/>user slider (per<br/>decision/refit-mask-grid-<br/>threshold ruling)"]
        fix_delete_mutation_stale_snapshot["<b>fix/delete-mutation-stale-snapshot</b><br/>run-mutation mutex<br/>(fix/run-mutation-mutex)<br/>still races on a stale<br/>snapshot: DELETE<br/>/api/runs, DELETE<br/>/api/sources and<br/>_persist_run supersede<br/>compute skip/protected<br/>under LOCK then rmtree<br/>OUTSIDE it, so a<br/>segment/refit registered<br/>into ACTIVE_RUN_MUTATIONS<br/>after the snapshot has its<br/>run dir deleted mid-<br/>mutation<br/>(feats/meta/source<br/>rmtree'd under it). Make<br/>victim selection +<br/>mutation-registration<br/>atomic under LOCK<br/>(DELETING_RUNS/SOURCES<br/>set, or atomic-rename<br/>victims to<br/>.trash/&lt;uuid&gt; under<br/>lock then delete outside),<br/>move the feats.f16<br/>precheck inside the lock<br/>block, and add a barrier<br/>concurrency regression<br/>test"]
        fix_weighted_quantile_plotting_position["<b>fix/weighted-quantile-plotting-position</b><br/>_weighted_quantile<br/>docstring claims it<br/>'matches torch.quantile<br/>when weights are equal'<br/>but its midpoint plotting-<br/>position CDF<br/>(cw-0.5w)/cw_n = (i-0.5)/n<br/>differs from<br/>torch.quantile's type-7<br/>linear i/(n-1) at small<br/>token counts (e.g.<br/>n=16,q=0.02 clamps to the<br/>min while torch<br/>interpolates xs[0..1]);<br/>refit display range is<br/>then more outlier-<br/>sensitive on small fg<br/>masks. Either make the<br/>weighted quantile reduce<br/>to torch.quantile at equal<br/>weights or correct the<br/>docstring's equivalence<br/>claim"]
        fix_upload_delete_source_phantom["<b>fix/upload-delete-source-phantom</b><br/>Duplicate upload racing<br/>DELETE /api/sources can<br/>re-register a source whose<br/>dir was just rmtree'd:<br/>upload_source's<br/>(SOURCES_DIR/sid).exists()<br/>check + dir handling run<br/>OUTSIDE LOCK and only<br/>SOURCES[sid]=rec is<br/>locked, so an identical-<br/>bytes re-upload taking the<br/>else-branch can insert sid<br/>into SOURCES after<br/>delete_source popped it<br/>and rmtree'd the dir -&gt;<br/>phantom source<br/>(list/workspace advertise<br/>it, create_runs 404s)<br/>until a restart re-scan.<br/>Pre-existing; surfaced by<br/>review-2026-07-07-review-<br/>fixes adversarial pass"]
        fix_vfr_decode_alignment["<b>fix/vfr-decode-alignment</b><br/>Frame alignment is NOT<br/>exact on VARIABLE-FRAME-<br/>RATE sources:<br/>seek_mode=approximate maps<br/>index-&gt;timestamp via<br/>AVERAGE fps, so phase-1<br/>NVDEC feats and SAM CPU<br/>re-decode can resolve<br/>different frames per index<br/>on VFR (CPU-proven in test<br/>s/test_frame_alignment.py;<br/>CFR closed/open-GOP are<br/>fine). seek_mode=exact<br/>crashes on stream-copy-<br/>trimmed uploads so is not<br/>an option. Fix: single<br/>decode path (decode<br/>sampled frames once, reuse<br/>for feats+SAM) or<br/>guarantee identical<br/>backend+seek. First GPU-<br/>validate NVDEC-vs-CPU<br/>divergence on the VFR<br/>fixture per the spike's<br/>plan"]
        fix_auth_hardening["<b>fix/auth-hardening</b><br/>Auth hardening follow-ups<br/>from review-2026-07-07<br/>security pass (all minor,<br/>single-user LAN tool):<br/>POST /api/login has no<br/>rate-limit/backoff so<br/>REPVIS_TOKEN is brute-<br/>forceable; token+cookie<br/>travel cleartext over HTTP<br/>(no Secure/TLS) — document<br/>TLS/SSH-tunnel requirement<br/>or add optional TLS;<br/>static/index.html leaves<br/>the #t= URL fragment<br/>unstripped in open mode<br/>(strip regardless)."]
        perf_phase2_sam_decode_tail["<b>perf/phase2-sam-decode-tail</b><br/>Attack the REAL phase-2<br/>tail found by the encode<br/>premise-check profile<br/>(1080p/1200f: SAM2 56.1s =<br/>64%, CPU source re-decode<br/>for SAM 15.2s = 17%,<br/>feats.f16 dump ~14s vs<br/>encode ~3%): parallelize<br/>SAM2 across GPUs or reuse<br/>phase-1 decoded frames for<br/>SAM (single-decode already<br/>shared post-vfr-fix),<br/>overlap the feats dump<br/>with render, and/or move<br/>SAM decode off CPU"]
        feat_saliency_artifact_tokens["<b>feat/saliency-artifact-tokens</b><br/>Auto-seed primary peak can<br/>land on background on<br/>near-uniform scenes:<br/>DINOv2-base high-norm<br/>artifact tokens hijack the<br/>saliency argmax (pre-<br/>existing, seen identically<br/>in OLD and NEW seeds<br/>during autoseed<br/>validation); suppress<br/>high-norm outlier tokens<br/>before the argmax or<br/>prefer a registers model"]
        fix_same_rid_mutation_marker["<b>fix/same-rid-mutation-marker</b><br/>ACTIVE_RUN_MUTATIONS is a<br/>set with no same-rid<br/>guard: two concurrent<br/>/segment|/refit on the<br/>SAME rid both pass the<br/>LOCK checks (only<br/>_active_run_ids is 409'd),<br/>and the first finisher's<br/>finally discard(rid)<br/>strips protection while<br/>the second is still<br/>queued/executing on EXEC<br/>-&gt; delete_runs/delete_s<br/>ource/_persist_run can<br/>rmtree the run dir under<br/>the in-flight mutation<br/>(executable repro in<br/>review; worst case a<br/>partially resurrected<br/>zombie run dir). Fix: 409<br/>when rid already in<br/>ACTIVE_RUN_MUTATIONS (both<br/>endpoints) + same-rid<br/>overlap regression test"]
        fix_segcache_device_sig["<b>fix/segcache-device-sig</b><br/>_SegCache sig<br/>(source_id,T) omits the<br/>device: sessions are<br/>pinned to their build-time<br/>GPU<br/>(Sam2VideoInferenceSession<br/>moves inputs to<br/>inference_device) while<br/>segment_and_render re-<br/>picks<br/>dev=select_devices()[0]<br/>per call, so a warm hit<br/>after device drift runs<br/>dev_new model x dev_old<br/>session -&gt; cross-device<br/>RuntimeError -&gt; 500<br/>(fail-fast, masks never<br/>wrong; self-heals cold;<br/>invisible on single-GPU<br/>pin). Fix:<br/>sig=(source_id,T,dev) so<br/>drift is a cold miss +<br/>unit test"]
        fix_create_runs_registration_window["<b>fix/create-runs-registration-window</b><br/>create_runs validates<br/>sources and mkdirs run<br/>dirs BEFORE registering<br/>the group in GROUPS under<br/>LOCK: a<br/>delete_runs/delete_source<br/>in that window rmtrees the<br/>nascent run dirs / just-<br/>validated source (graceful<br/>group error, no corruption<br/>- task finally cleans up).<br/>Move validation+mkdir+regi<br/>stration into one LOCK<br/>critical section"]
        fix_autoseed_negative_gate["<b>fix/autoseed-negative-gate</b><br/>Reviewer open question:<br/>the auto-seed border<br/>NEGATIVE prompt is planted<br/>ungated - on a subject<br/>filling the frame to the<br/>borders, patch mean ~=<br/>subject prototype forces<br/>the lowest-saliency border<br/>patch ONTO the subject (a<br/>constructed counterexample<br/>to the 'never worse than<br/>single-point' claim; the<br/>n=5 eval had one<br/>fill_frame clip). Either<br/>gate the negative<br/>symmetrically (plant only<br/>if its feature is cosine-<br/>closer to mean than to<br/>peak 1) or downgrade the<br/>claim to the empirical<br/>5-clip statement; decide<br/>after feat/saliency-<br/>artifact-tokens lands<br/>(same function)"]
        perf_multi_gpu_sam["<b>perf/multi-gpu-sam</b><br/>The dominant phase-2 lever<br/>after the dump overlap:<br/>SAM2 segmentation is ~40s<br/>of ~52s phase-2 wall<br/>(64-72%) and is single-GPU<br/>sequential temporal<br/>propagation; investigate<br/>multi-GPU SAM parallelism<br/>(per-source fan-out for<br/>joint runs first, then<br/>intra-clip conditioning-<br/>point splits) — needs<br/>&gt;=2 free GPUs to<br/>validate"]

        fix_stale_vfr_run_migration["<b>fix/stale-vfr-run-migration</b><br/>Runs created BEFORE the<br/>positional-decode fix<br/>carry masks/feats under<br/>the old avg-fps timestamp<br/>index definition; a click<br/>on such a run re-decodes<br/>positionally and misaligns<br/>with the stored feats.         perf_segcache_byte_budget["<b>perf/segcache-byte-budget</b><br/>_SegCache bounds entries<br/>(LRU=2) not bytes: a<br/>cached session holds all<br/>frames as 1024^2 float32<br/>(~11.3GB @900 frames) +<br/>vision features, so worst<br/>case 20GB+ host RAM; bound<br/>the cache by a byte budget<br/>(estimate per-session size<br/>at put) instead of entry<br/>count"]
        perf_sparse_decode_full_walk["<b>perf/sparse-decode-full-walk</b><br/>Positional sequential<br/>decode walks the whole<br/>stream (and per-unit<br/>prefixes, and a second<br/>full CPU walk for SAM) so<br/>max_frames sparse sampling<br/>no longer bounds decode<br/>work on long videos.<br/>Probe-gated exact-indexed<br/>routing was implemented<br/>then REFUTED by<br/>adversarial review (index-<br/>set-dependent silent<br/>misalignment on spliced<br/>sources; see wip/exact-<br/>decode-router + the splice<br/>characterization test).<br/>Remaining sound<br/>directions: (a) per-<br/>(file,indices,chunking)<br/>full-comparison<br/>certification enabling<br/>exact only for byte-<br/>verified REPEAT SAM re-<br/>decodes, (b) one<br/>sequential decode pass per<br/>source fanned out to units<br/>(kills the prefix re-walk<br/>multiplier, no trust in<br/>seeks needed), (c) overlap<br/>the SAM CPU walk under<br/>phase-1 GPU work for<br/>initial runs"]
        fix_autoseed_outlier_subject_coverage["<b>fix/autoseed-outlier-subject-coverage</b><br/>The MAD norm filter's<br/>'never excludes a real<br/>subject' claim is only<br/>tested on the artifact<br/>side: add a fixture where<br/>a small high-contrast REAL<br/>foreground is itself a<br/>norm outlier (and a<br/>textureless close-up), and<br/>decide the policy if the<br/>subject is demonstrably<br/>excludable; also<br/>skip/relocate the border<br/>negative if it collides<br/>with a positive cell"]

        fix_exact_decode_silent_misalignment["<b>fix/exact-decode-silent-misalignment</b><br/>Probe-gated exact-indexed<br/>decode silently misaligns<br/>on mid-stream-<br/>discontinuous sources<br/>(splice/reconnect):<br/>torchcodec exact<br/>get_frames_at resolution<br/>is INDEX-SET-dependent<br/>(dense correct, sparse<br/>strides collapse onto the<br/>pre-splice frame, no<br/>exception), so no fixed<br/>probe set can certify the<br/>production index set —<br/>contain by removing the<br/>exact routing (router<br/>preserved on wip/exact-<br/>decode-router), add a<br/>splice fixture +<br/>characterization test<br/>recording the refutation"]

## Tasks

| ID | Title | Status | Round | Deps | Anchor |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `chore/decode-codec-coverage` | Frame-alignment fixtures are all x264+MP4 but _VIDEO_EXTS accepts webm/mkv/avi: verify the positional decode's pre-roll skip (pts>=begin_stream epsilon) and index semantics on VP9/webm, mkv, and HEVC open-GOP fixtures (fable review residual: positional definition is demuxer-neutral, the pre-roll判定 is not) | ⬜ pending | — | — | — |
| `fix/autoseed-outlier-subject-coverage` | The MAD norm filter's 'never excludes a real subject' claim is only tested on the artifact side: add a fixture where a small high-contrast REAL foreground is itself a norm outlier (and a textureless close-up), and decide the policy if the subject is demonstrably excludable; also skip/relocate the border negative if it collides with a positive cell | ⬜ pending | 2026-07-10-router-refuted | — | — |
| `fix/stale-vfr-run-migration` | Runs created BEFORE the positional-decode fix carry masks/feats under the old avg-fps timestamp index definition; a click on such a run re-decodes positionally and misaligns with the stored feats. Add a decode-semantics marker to meta.json and detect/flag stale runs in the workspace (prompt re-run), or auto-invalidate their seg | ⬜ pending | — | — | — |
| `perf/multi-gpu-sam` | The dominant phase-2 lever after the dump overlap: SAM2 segmentation is ~40s of ~52s phase-2 wall (64-72%) and is single-GPU sequential temporal propagation; investigate multi-GPU SAM parallelism (per-source fan-out for joint runs first, then intra-clip conditioning-point splits) — needs >=2 free GPUs to validate | ⬜ pending | — | — | — |
| `perf/segcache-byte-budget` | _SegCache bounds entries (LRU=2) not bytes: a cached session holds all frames as 1024^2 float32 (~11.3GB @900 frames) + vision features, so worst case 20GB+ host RAM; bound the cache by a byte budget (estimate per-session size at put) instead of entry count | ⬜ pending | — | — | — |
| `perf/sparse-decode-full-walk` | Positional sequential decode walks the whole stream (and per-unit prefixes, and a second full CPU walk for SAM) so max_frames sparse sampling no longer bounds decode work on long videos. Probe-gated exact-indexed routing was implemented then REFUTED by adversarial review (index-set-dependent silent misalignment on spliced sources; see wip/exact-decode-router + the splice characterization test). Remaining sound directions: (a) per-(file,indices,chunking) full-comparison certification enabling exact only for byte-verified REPEAT SAM re-decodes, (b) one sequential decode pass per source fanned out to units (kills the prefix re-walk multiplier, no trust in seeks needed), (c) overlap the SAM CPU walk under phase-1 GPU work for initial runs | ⬜ pending | 2026-07-10-router-refuted | — | — |

| `fix/autoseed-negative-gate` | Reviewer open question: the auto-seed border NEGATIVE prompt is planted ungated - on a subject filling the frame to the borders, patch mean ~= subject prototype forces the lowest-saliency border patch ONTO the subject (a constructed counterexample to the 'never worse than single-point' claim; the n=5 eval had one fill_frame clip). Either gate the negative symmetrically (plant only if its feature is cosine-closer to mean than to peak 1) or downgrade the claim to the empirical 5-clip statement; decide after feat/saliency-artifact-tokens lands (same function) | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-backlog-zero | feat/saliency-artifact-tokens | — |
| `fix/create-runs-registration-window` | create_runs validates sources and mkdirs run dirs BEFORE registering the group in GROUPS under LOCK: a delete_runs/delete_source in that window rmtrees the nascent run dirs / just-validated source (graceful group error, no corruption - task finally cleans up). Move validation+mkdir+registration into one LOCK critical section | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-backlog-zero | — | — |
| `fix/delete-mutation-stale-snapshot` | run-mutation mutex (fix/run-mutation-mutex) still races on a stale snapshot: DELETE /api/runs, DELETE /api/sources and _persist_run supersede compute skip/protected under LOCK then rmtree OUTSIDE it, so a segment/refit registered into ACTIVE_RUN_MUTATIONS after the snapshot has its run dir deleted mid-mutation (feats/meta/source rmtree'd under it). Make victim selection + mutation-registration atomic under LOCK (DELETING_RUNS/SOURCES set, or atomic-rename victims to .trash/<uuid> under lock then delete outside), move the feats.f16 precheck inside the lock block, and add a barrier concurrency regression test | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
| `fix/exact-decode-silent-misalignment` | Probe-gated exact-indexed decode silently misaligns on mid-stream-discontinuous sources (splice/reconnect): torchcodec exact get_frames_at resolution is INDEX-SET-dependent (dense correct, sparse strides collapse onto the pre-splice frame, no exception), so no fixed probe set can certify the production index set — contain by removing the exact routing (router preserved on wip/exact-decode-router), add a splice fixture + characterization test recording the refutation | ✅ done | 2026-07-10-router-refuted | — | — |
| `fix/refit-soft-weight-mask` | Replace refit's hard adaptive_avg_pool2d(mask,grid)>0.5 fg-token gate with fg-fraction SOFT WEIGHTING (weighted PCA over grid tokens) so thin structures survive the color refit; no hardcoded threshold, no user slider (per decision/refit-mask-grid-threshold ruling) | ✅ done | 2026-07-07-review-fixes | — | — |
| `fix/run-mutation-mutex` | segment/refit re-render is not excluded from DELETE /api/runs, DELETE /api/sources, or (source,model) supersede — a concurrent delete can rmtree run_dir/feats/masks/source mid-render; track in-progress run mutations and gate destructive ops | ✅ done | 2026-07-07-review-fixes | — | — |
| `fix/sam-failure-silent-fallback` | SAM2 exception/empty mask is hidden as all-foreground + available=False, which also strips the client's click controls (no recovery) and lets refine failures overwrite good masks; distinguish error vs empty, keep controls, don't clobber on refine failure, add failure-mode tests (monkeypatch + mask-ratio asserts) | ✅ done | 2026-07-07-review-fixes | — | — |
| `fix/same-rid-mutation-marker` | ACTIVE_RUN_MUTATIONS is a set with no same-rid guard: two concurrent /segment|/refit on the SAME rid both pass the LOCK checks (only _active_run_ids is 409'd), and the first finisher's finally discard(rid) strips protection while the second is still queued/executing on EXEC -> delete_runs/delete_source/_persist_run can rmtree the run dir under the in-flight mutation (executable repro in review; worst case a partially resurrected zombie run dir). Fix: 409 when rid already in ACTIVE_RUN_MUTATIONS (both endpoints) + same-rid overlap regression test | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-backlog-zero | — | — |
| `fix/segcache-device-sig` | _SegCache sig (source_id,T) omits the device: sessions are pinned to their build-time GPU (Sam2VideoInferenceSession moves inputs to inference_device) while segment_and_render re-picks dev=select_devices()[0] per call, so a warm hit after device drift runs dev_new model x dev_old session -> cross-device RuntimeError -> 500 (fail-fast, masks never wrong; self-heals cold; invisible on single-GPU pin). Fix: sig=(source_id,T,dev) so drift is a cold miss + unit test | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-backlog-zero | — | — |
| `fix/segment-click-frame-idx` | Segment refine clicks always seed frame 0: client sends [x,y,label] with no frame, sam.segment uses seed_frame=0 -> a click on a later frame maps coords to frame 0 and corrupts SAM propagation. Thread frame idx (point schema + per-frame prompting) | ✅ done | 2026-07-07-review-fixes | — | — |
| `fix/segment-point-validation` | _parse_points accepts NaN/Infinity/out-of-bounds coords (shape-only check); validate finite + within the run's WxH (+ frame idx) and reject 4xx before reaching SAM2 | ✅ done | 2026-07-07-review-fixes | — | — |
| `fix/shared-model-load-lock` | extractor and SAM2 from_pretrained race on torch global default dtype: their _load_lock objects are separate and run_group warms both concurrently (pipeline 534-535); share ONE global model-construction lock or sequence extractor->SAM warm | ✅ done | 2026-07-07-review-fixes | — | — |
| `fix/upload-delete-source-phantom` | Duplicate upload racing DELETE /api/sources can re-register a source whose dir was just rmtree'd: upload_source's (SOURCES_DIR/sid).exists() check + dir handling run OUTSIDE LOCK and only SOURCES[sid]=rec is locked, so an identical-bytes re-upload taking the else-branch can insert sid into SOURCES after delete_source popped it and rmtree'd the dir -> phantom source (list/workspace advertise it, create_runs 404s) until a restart re-scan. Pre-existing; surfaced by review-2026-07-07-review-fixes adversarial pass | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
| `fix/vfr-decode-alignment` | Frame alignment is NOT exact on VARIABLE-FRAME-RATE sources: seek_mode=approximate maps index->timestamp via AVERAGE fps, so phase-1 NVDEC feats and SAM CPU re-decode can resolve different frames per index on VFR (CPU-proven in tests/test_frame_alignment.py; CFR closed/open-GOP are fine). seek_mode=exact crashes on stream-copy-trimmed uploads so is not an option. Fix: single decode path (decode sampled frames once, reuse for feats+SAM) or guarantee identical backend+seek. First GPU-validate NVDEC-vs-CPU divergence on the VFR fixture per the spike's plan | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
| `fix/weighted-quantile-plotting-position` | _weighted_quantile docstring claims it 'matches torch.quantile when weights are equal' but its midpoint plotting-position CDF (cw-0.5w)/cw_n = (i-0.5)/n differs from torch.quantile's type-7 linear i/(n-1) at small token counts (e.g. n=16,q=0.02 clamps to the min while torch interpolates xs[0..1]); refit display range is then more outlier-sensitive on small fg masks. Either make the weighted quantile reduce to torch.quantile at equal weights or correct the docstring's equivalence claim | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
| `perf/bench-giant-huge-compile` | Benchmark FP8/compile gains for dinov2-giant and dinov3-vith16plus (huge+ compile gain is only estimated ~+15%) | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
| `perf/phase2-sam-decode-tail` | Attack the REAL phase-2 tail found by the encode premise-check profile (1080p/1200f: SAM2 56.1s = 64%, CPU source re-decode for SAM 15.2s = 17%, feats.f16 dump ~14s vs encode ~3%): parallelize SAM2 across GPUs or reuse phase-1 decoded frames for SAM (single-decode already shared post-vfr-fix), overlap the feats dump with render, and/or move SAM decode off CPU | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-backlog-zero | — | — |
| `perf/sam-session-cache` | Persist the Sam2VideoInferenceSession / vision features per run so +/- click re-segmentation skips recomputation and cuts click latency | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
| `spike/fp8-attention` | Evaluate FP8 attention for a true ~2x forward speedup versus its fidelity risk (currently unshipped) | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-backlog-zero | — | — |
| `spike/frame-alignment-check` | Verify 'frame alignment is exact': phase-1 GPU-decode vs SAM CPU re-decode both use seek_mode=approximate and tests only check index-length; add checksum/hash verification + open-GOP/VFR fixtures, or reuse one decode path | ✅ done | 2026-07-08-parallel-sweep | — | — |
